failures.evidence: KB lists slide headings/section titles rather than true core topics. Missing 'Color Theory' (named in Slide 1 title) and 'AI Training Data / Bias' (Slide 4 topic). Some entries like 'Compare (Optional)' are minor sub-headings, not core topics.

KB lists 'Creating YOUR Mood Board' and 'The Basics of Mood Boards' which are the headings from Slide 5 under 'Lesson Objectives'. These are topic headings, not actual objective statements (e.g., 'Students will be able to...'). The source does not expand these into proper objectives, so this is the best available extraction, but they are incomplete as learning objectives.

List captures legitimate topics (Game Ideation, brainstorming, narrative, gameplay mechanics) but polluted with structural/UI elements like 'Find Mural HERE', 'Portfolio Evidence — Lesson 9', 'What you built + how you used AI', and 'Let's Discuss' which are slide headings/calls-to-action, not substantive topics.

Key activities (Starter, AI vs Manual comparison, CapCut demo, Activity 14.1 build steps) are present. However, speaker notes are mixed into the activity description, 'Lower effort to produce' is omitted from the AI-Generated list, and several CapCut demo features from Slide 8 (Interface overview, Upload gameplay clips, etc.) are not listed.

Both actual learning objectives from Slide 4 are correctly captured. However, KB includes two additional items ('I produced a structured 15–20 second trailer...' and 'I edited confidently in CapCut...') that originate from the Slide 16 self-assessment, not the learning objectives section.

KB videos field contains only one entry. Source references at least two video links: Slide 10 ('Check out this Video with the clear steps') and Slide 15 ('Check out this video for tips on how to create more cinematic videos'). The Runway ML demo video referenced in Slide 6 is also absent from the videos field.

The source markdown for slide 2 references 'Click here to start the assessment' and slide 9 references 'Recap from Term 2's Core Pillars' as hyperlinks, but the actual URLs are not rendered in the markdown export. KB includes three URLs which may have been extracted from the PPTX file directly, but they cannot be verified against the provided source content. URL accuracy cannot be confirmed.

The two genuine objectives from slide 5 are captured correctly. However, a third item ('I compared generative AI and agentic AI and justified when AI should or should not be used in game development.') is misattributed — it comes from the slide 21 self-assessment section, not the learning objectives slide.

Source slide 3 explicitly labels 'Agentic AI in Healthcare' as a video ('Check out this video 🔗 Agentic AI in Healthcare'). This is captured in the resources field with its Drive URL, but the videos array is empty. The content exists in the KB but is misclassified.

Real topics (ethical marketing, launch announcement creation, AI tagline drafting, ethical check) are present. However, the list also includes MCQ distractor answers ('AI decides what is okay to promise', 'AI is responsible if players are misled') and granular activity steps as topics, diluting and misrepresenting the actual core topics.

Both real objectives (Apply Ethical Judgment, Structured Launch Announcement) are captured correctly. However, a third item 'My Success Criteria / Self-Assessment' is included — this is the slide 20 section header, not a learning objective. Two genuine objectives present, one spurious item added.

Source slide 10 explicitly references a video tutorial (🎥). The video URL is captured in the resources field (Drive link) rather than the videos array. The videos array is empty when there is at least one video reference in the source.

Main themes are present but the list is excessively granular — individual slide bullet points (e.g., 'Groups similar feedback quickly', 'Detect repetition across responses') and framework labels ('OECD Mapping') are listed as core topics. Actual high-level topics (feedback analysis, iteration planning, AI vs human judgment) are present but buried in noise.

The two actual learning objectives from Slide 4 are correctly captured. However, the KB also includes three items from Slide 15's self-assessment rubric (All/Most/Some criteria), which belong to success_criteria, not learning_objectives. Mixing field types reduces accuracy.

KB lists a Google Slides URL for 'Empathise → Define → Ideate → Prototype', which corresponds to the Term 2 reference on Slide 11. However, this URL does not appear explicitly in the source markdown — it may be an embedded hyperlink from the original PPTX. Cannot verify the URL is correct from the source provided. Link text is grounded in source; URL cannot be confirmed.

KB includes relevant terms like 'Portfolio', 'Evidence', 'Curation' but lists extraneous slide section headers ('Deepen – Focused Build Time', 'MCQ 11.1', etc.) and misses key thematic topics from the lesson plan: documentation, design communication, design cycle reflection.

Lesson 11 activities are present (Starter, Activity 11.1, 11.2, Portfolio Entry 13, reflection plenary) but the description is heavily mixed with Lesson 12 content (Portfolio Entries 14A, 14B, 15) from the exemplar file, making attribution unclear and the Lesson 11 content hard to isolate.

KB lists only 'My Success Criteria / Self-Assessment' which is a slide prompt, not an objective. Source has two proper objectives: 'Evidence Selection — Select and organise evidence from each stage of the Product Development Cycle' and 'Design Communication — Create clear captions and reflections to communicate the design process.' Both are missing.
KB title is 'Lesson 11 – Learning Check' which is a slide section header. Source lesson plan title is 'Lesson 11: Documentation & Portfolio Building — Telling the Design Story'.

Most substantive topics are captured (Bartle's Four Player Types, empathy mapping, persona creation, AI bias, etc.), but the list includes template placeholder text ('Type Here') and slide section headers that are not topics (e.g., 'Example Persona', 'AI Draft & Bias Check', 'Jump into Endstar').

The first 2 objectives match the lesson plan exactly. Items 3–5 ('I created a clear persona...', 'I explained one design decision...', 'I used AI...') are self-assessment success criteria from slide 16, not learning objectives. Teacher note says 'retain the full 5-item list in the written plan', suggesting the inclusion was intentional, but the mixing of objectives and self-assessment criteria is a category error.

KB lists 5 URLs including 4 Google Drive links plausibly corresponding to resources mentioned in the PD Week 1 file (Technical Support Guides, Design Briefs, Assessment Guides, Learning Schedule). However, the Wikipedia UX link is not explicitly present in the source, and individual Drive folder URLs cannot be fully verified from the source markdown.

The second video URL (QvRZuHQBTps) is also not verifiable from the provided source text. URLs may be correct but cannot be confirmed.

KB includes valid topics (Game Design, Rewritten Brief, Team Roles, Constraints, Success Criteria, Research Insights) derived from slide content, but also includes slide-specific labels not in the lesson plan source (MCQ 4.1/4.2/4.3, Extension tasks, Endstar Warm-Up). Source lesson plan does not list these as core topics. Mix of accurate and out-of-scope items.

'Portfolio and documentation' captures the upload/portfolio aspect but misses the primary activity type — creative design work (moodboard creation). A more accurate classification would include 'Creative/Design Project'.

Interactions and Prototyping tools are inferred from slide 11's 'Dynamic Interactions' and 'Rapid Prototyping'. 'Logic' and 'Mechanics' are conceptual terms from the lesson, not explicitly named as distinct Endstar tool categories in source.

'Polish' partly fits (the pitch/presentation is being polished), but 'Game expansion' mischaracterises the lesson — the game itself is already built; this lesson is entirely about finalising and rehearsing the showcase pitch. Should be something like 'Presentation preparation and rehearsal'.

The actual tiered criteria from slide 12 are captured correctly, but the list also includes 'AI as an Assistant' (a section heading, not a criterion) and 'Question 2' / 'Question 3' (discussion prompts from slide 15). These misclassified items contaminate an otherwise correct field.

big_question.evidence: KB exactly matches source slide 4: 'How can we promote our game clearly and honestly without misleading players?'

uae_link.evidence: KB accurately captures the UAE AI Ethics Guidelines content from slide 3. It also includes the reflection question from the same slide, which is a minor over-inclusion but not inaccurate — both originate from the same slide context.

endstar_tools.evidence: Source contains no reference to Endstar tools; KB field is empty. No penalty.

keywords.evidence: Keywords listed (audience, player, gameplay, AI, launch, etc.) are all present in source. However, prominent lesson-specific terms are missing: 'tagline', 'ethical', 'marketing', 'announcement', 'honest' — the most central vocabulary of this lesson.

activity_type.evidence: Classified as 'Peer testing and feedback'. Peer review does appear as an extension task (slide 18), but the primary activity is creation-based (Activity 5.1 — drafting a launch announcement). The label partially describes one activity type but misses the dominant mode.

assessment_signals.evidence: KB captures the three-level self-assessment rubric from slide 20 (All/Most/Some). These are the lesson's explicit assessment indicators and are accurately reproduced.

curriculum_alignment.evidence: Source slide 5 notes list UK D&T KS3 1d, AQA 3.1.1, IB Criterion C, CSTA 3A-IC-25 — all captured in KB. OECD mappings (K3.3, K4.1, K5.4) from slides 21–23 are also correctly included.

ai_focus.evidence: KB captures key AI themes: responsible use of Notebook LM/Gemini, 'AI suggests, humans decide' principle, AI in marketing risks, and AI literacy OECD codes. Aligns with source's emphasis on ethical AI use throughout.

artifacts.evidence: Source slide 15 references 'Portfolio Entry 22: Launch Announcement Draft'. KB lists both the numbered entry and a generic variant. Core artifact is correctly identified.

grade_band.evidence: Source content does not explicitly state a grade band. KB asserts G9–G10 which is plausible for this curriculum level, but cannot be confirmed or denied from the provided source excerpt.

document_sources.evidence: KB lists 'term2\Teacher Resources\Curriculum Content\Week 3\Lesson 5.md', which exactly matches the source file path in the content header.

lesson_title.evidence: KB title 'Lesson 22 – Polishing Your Pitch' matches source slide 2 exactly.

learning_objectives.evidence: Both objectives — 'Review your presentation checklist' and 'Start creating your Showcase presentation' — match slide 5 verbatim.

description_of_activities.evidence: KB captures the Ana critical-friend activity (slide 7), all three Ana prompts, the reflection questions, Activity 1 finalise-presentation text (slide 10), the three-tier criteria checklist (slide 12), and the teaching note (slide 11). Comprehensive and accurate.

core_topics.evidence: All ten listed topics are slide headings/section labels present in the source: Ask AI – Persuasive Communication, Getting Showcase Ready, AI as an Assistant, Portfolio Evidence, Assessment For Learning, Game Development, AI Literacy (OECD K1.3), Let's Discuss, etc.

teacher_notes.evidence: Single speaker note on slide 11 ('Teaching Note: Have students complete the activities and then upload their progress to classkick.') captured correctly.

slides.evidence: All 15 slides present with content matching source text. No slides missing or fabricated.

videos.evidence: Source contains no video URLs or references; empty array is appropriate.

resources.evidence: Source contains no resource links or external references; empty array is appropriate.

success_criteria.evidence: The actual tiered criteria from slide 12 are captured correctly, but the list also includes 'AI as an Assistant' (a section heading, not a criterion) and 'Question 2' / 'Question 3' (discussion prompts from slide 15). These misclassified items contaminate an otherwise correct field.

big_question.evidence: Source contains no explicit Big Question field; empty string is appropriate.

uae_link.evidence: Source contains no UAE link or connection; empty string is appropriate.

endstar_tools.evidence: KB lists 'Logic' but the source makes no mention of any Endstar tool called Logic. The only AI tool referenced is Ana. This appears fabricated.

keywords.evidence: All listed keywords (audience, testing, gameplay, feedback, portfolio, reflection, collaboration, assessment, presentation, AI, game design, strategy) are grounded in source content and appear across multiple slides.

activity_type.evidence: 'Polish' partly fits (the pitch/presentation is being polished), but 'Game expansion' mischaracterises the lesson — the game itself is already built; this lesson is entirely about finalising and rehearsing the showcase pitch. Should be something like 'Presentation preparation and rehearsal'.

assessment_signals.evidence: All four MCQ assessment items (GD-22.1, GD-22.2, AI-22.1, AI-22.2) from slides 13–14 are captured accurately, including answer options.

curriculum_alignment.evidence: OECD K1.3 is correctly identified (slides 3 and 14), but the first entry text is truncated to 'OECD K1.3)' and neither entry includes the full description 'Choosing when NOT to use AI'. Content is present but incomplete.

ai_focus.evidence: KB only lists 'AI Literacy (OECD K1.3)' but source has two substantial AI strands: (1) Ask AI – Persuasive Communication (slide 4) and (2) Ana as AI assistant / critical friend (slide 7). Missing these two aspects is a significant omission.

artifacts.evidence: Source slide 11 explicitly names a portfolio artifact — 'Portfolio Evidence — Lesson 22: What you built + how you used AI' — yet KB artifacts array is empty.

grade_band.evidence: Source does not state a grade band; 'Level 11' on slide 1 refers to the programme level, not a school grade. Cannot verify G9–G10 from the provided source content.

document_sources.evidence: Listed path 'term1\Lessons Resources\Lesson Decks (Slides)\Explorer's Programme - Lesson 22.md' matches the source file header exactly.
